notes_for_human_reviewer: CALIBRATION NOTE (2026-05-05): first run scored stated_vs_actual_gap_recognition at 2 — Claude reads the moralised framing (decisions are 'a clear violation') as itself a non-observational diagnosis, even though the observations themselves are correct. Defensible: the chapter's framing is that the stated structure is a hypothesis and observed behaviour is the data, not that observed behaviour is a violation. Adjusted from 4 +/-1 to 3 +/-2 (allows 1-5). OVERSTEP FAILURE MODE — the canonical anti-pattern Chapter 6 explicitly warns against. The learner has correctly diagnosed the gap (stated_vs_actual scores 4 — most of the right observations, just no typing) but proposes to fix it themselves at senior level. Five proposed moves all overstep: emailing the sponsor directly to call out his failure, editorialising in SC minutes ('Committee has not made any substantive decisions'), confronting K. Bell, lecturing the Head of PMO on RACI standards, pressuring the CMO. Fires role_boundary_respect cap unambiguously — multiple direct interventions with senior stakeholders to fix governance. Tests whether the grader catches confidence-but-wrong-role. Sponsor/SC typing is missing ('SC is functionally non-functional', 'sponsor is disengaged' — describes but does not type per chapter's specific categories) — fires the untyped cap. The structure of the memo looks proactive but the substance is exactly what the chapter says NOT to do.
